Description:
How can designers get closer to code without losing their craft?

In this conversation, Elijah King (VS Code Design) and Thoa Nguyen (Microsoft Foundry design) explore practical AI-assisted workflows for designers: using VS Code, GitHub Copilot, plan mode, parallel sessions, and design systems in real repos. They discuss where Figma still shines, how code-based prototyping changes collaboration with engineers, and what GenUI could mean for the future of design roles.

If you’re a designer curious about working more directly in engineering workflows, this one’s for you.

🔎 Chapters: 00:00 Big question: where do designers fit with agents? 00:35 Intro: Eli + Thoa 00:59 Keeping up with rapid AI tool changes 02:13 Tools in practice: Copilot CLI, chat, models, plan mode 03:42 Prompting strategies: one-shot vs step-by-step sessions 04:53 Parallel sessions + worktrees for design variants 06:10 Why prototyping in VS Code reveals real edge cases 07:40 Figma’s role in an AI-assisted workflow 10:23 Designer–engineer collaboration and role overlap 12:49 Vibe Foundry: a design playground in code 16:22 What is GenUI? 20:21 Future of design roles in agent-driven experiences 21:04 Wrap-up and thanks

🎙️ Featuring: Elijah King (https://www.linkedin.com/in/elijahwilliamking/), Thoa Nguyen (https://www.linkedin.com/in/thoanguyen/)

#vscode #design #ai
Title: Why Design feels different
Author: Visual Studio Code
